posts.text:
Huge thanks to NP Digital for inviting me to speak at Full Circle Conference! It was a joy to speak with Nikki Lam about all things agentic and to connect with so many incredibly smart and ambitious delegates and fellow speakers!

Hat's off to Basil H., Ronnie Malewski, and Mike Morrison and the whole NP Digital team for making my first time speaking in 🍁 Canada 🍁 truly magical. 

The day was filled with great sessions from Mitch Joel, Erin Elofson, Jonelle (Francois) Ricketts, Neil Patel, Tedde van Gelderen. And I had great time speaking with delegates about how the web is developing and how it's effecting their businesses and clients. 

I spoke to lots of delegates who were interested in the agentic strides that we're making at Wix and Wix Studio, so if you're a new follower who's interested, check out our collection of resources on agent readiness at the link in the comments. See you next time Toronto 🍁

What an absolute pleasure to speak at Harvard Business School today! 

I spoke about AI search, SEO, the agentic web and how to future proof brands for this new era of discovery. 

There were some incredible discussions with MBA students about careers, considerations for new businesses and even met labrador :)

Huge thanks to Shira Amit for organizing the session and for such a warm welcome! 🙏
Track the RIGHT prompts y'all. Not every prompt, the right ones.

Tracking your #AI prompts? Let's make sure the math is mathing 🤓
There is a lot of noise right now about prompt tracking and how to squeeze real value out of it. But let's be real, sometimes it comes down to the prompts themselves.

Tracking AI performance isn't just about having data; it's about prompts that give you *actionable* data so you can actually do something with it.

Here's my top 3 tips for prompt tracking efficiency:
1️⃣ Evict the False Positives: If a prompt is giving you "success" signals that don't translate to brand visibility, it's just clutter. Get it out of your reports.

2️⃣ Sync Your Tools and Your Timeframes: Make sure your prompts actually align with what your tools are capable of measuring, and that the tracking duration matches the task. Don't track a marathon like it's a sprint.

3️⃣ The "Action" Litmus Test: This is the golden rule. If you aren't going to change your strategy based on a metric, don't track it. We don't need more "vanity metrics"; we need insights that move the needle.

I've broken down the full "how-to" on prompt tracking over in my webinar with chima mmeje🏳️‍🌈 and now my latest piece on the Moz blog. Check it out and let's get those dashboards working for us, not against us.

Read the full guide here: 
https://lnkd.in/edYeQtu4
